intended_use: The SIGNA 7.0T system is a whole-body magnetic resonance scanner designed to support high resolution, high signal-to-noise ratio, and short scan times. It is indicated for use as a diagnostic imaging
intended_use_full: The SIGNA 7.0T system is a whole-body magnetic resonance scanner designed to support high resolution, high signal-to-noise ratio, and short scan times. It is indicated for use as a diagnostic imaging device to produce axial, sagittal, coronal, and oblique images, spectroscopic images, parametric maps, and/or spectra, dynamic images of the structures and/or functions of the head and extremities. The images produced by the SIGNA 7.0T system reflects the spatial distribution or molecular environment of nuclei exhibiting magnetic resonance. These images and/or spectra when interpreted by a trained physician yield information that may assist in diagnosis.
ground_truth: Same subject images scanned on the predicate (3.0T) device served as the comparison reference
other_metrics: Image diagnostic quality and usability evaluated qualitatively by 4 U.S. board-certified radiologists; PNS (peripheral nerve stimulation) limits determined via direct determination study with adult human volunteers
